steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The goal is to "specify where" Hadramphus tuberculatus was identified. The answer must name a location for that species only.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'In 1974, a living specimen of Catagonus wagneri was found in Argentina.': Names the wrong species (Catagonus wagneri, not Hadramphus tuberculatus). ✗
  • Choice B — 'A living specimen of Hadramphus tuberculatus, once believed to be extinct, was identified in 2004.': Names the correct species but gives only the year, not a location. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Previously believed to be extinct, a living specimen of Hadramphus tuberculatus was identified in New Zealand.': Names the correct species and includes the location (New Zealand) from the notes. ✓
  • Choice D — 'Examples of Lazarus species can be found in Hadramphus tuberculatus as well as Catagonus wagneri.': Describes the concept of Lazarus species without specifying where Hadramphus tuberculatus was found. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
